title: 10 best herbal creams for itchy eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Pot marigold creams contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in pot marigold creams help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eyes, and also soothe and calm irritated skin.

Wound-healing property: Pot marigold creams contain saponins, which have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue repair, helping to speed up the recovery process of damaged eye tissues and reducing itchiness.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The soothing and cooling effects of Calendula reduce redness and swelling, providing quick relief from itchy eyes. Its ability to calm and protect the delicate skin around the eyes makes it an effective remedy for minor irritations and allergies, such as conjunctivitis and dry eye syndrome. Regular use of Calendula creams can also help to prevent future occurrences.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help with itchy eyes by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.

Naphthoquinones: These compounds, specifically triterpenoid naphthoquinones, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can alleviate itchy eyes by reducing the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Carotenoids: Carotenoids like beta-carotene and lutein present in Calendula officinalis have antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
(Optional) Seeds: While not as commonly used as flowers or leaves, the seeds of Calendula officinalis may also be used to make creams for itchy eyes due to their potential anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30 grams of dried calendula officinalis flowers in 1 liter of carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 2-3 hours at a low heat setting.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Mix the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of shea butter in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat and stir until the beeswax and shea butter are fully melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and redness in itchy eyes, providing quick relief from discomfort.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial and antifungal compounds, including salicylic acid and phenolic acids, help combat infections that can cause itchy eyes and prevent further irritation.

Soothing and moisturizing property: Aloe vera's gel-like substance provides a soothing and moisturizing barrier on the eye surface, calming itchiness and reducing dryness, making it an effective natural treatment for itchy eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. Additionally, aloe vera's hydrating properties help to calm and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itchiness and discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate itchy eyes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone derivative with anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which helps to reduce redness and itching in itchy eyes.
Aloe-β-sitosterol: Aloe-β-sitosterol is a phytosterol that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
Glucomannan: Glucomannan is a polysaccharide that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which helps to reduce swelling and itching in itchy eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves is used due to its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce itching and inflammation.
Leaves: The extract from the leaves' outer layer, also known as the sap or latex, is used for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help combat infections.
Leaves: The juice from the leaves is used due to its moisturizing and calming properties that help soothe and hydrate the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of aloe vera gel from 200g of aloe vera leaves after 30 minutes of soaking in cold water.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter in a double boiler at 180f for 10 minutes.
Combine 30g of coconut oil with 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of essential oil in a separate container.
Gradually add the oil mixture to the beeswax mixture and stir until fully incorporated after 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and calming irritated tissues.
Antihistamine: Chamomile contains natural antihistamines that can help alleviate itching and scratching sensations caused by allergies or irritants, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Soothing and Calming: The calming and soothing properties of chamomile help to reduce stress and anxiety-related itchiness in the eyes, promoting relaxation and comfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and bisabolol, have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. This natural cream calms irritated tissues and provides relief from itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or environmental factors. Regular use of chamomile creams can help to alleviate discomfort and promote healthy eye function, making it a popular remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an antihistamine and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps in reducing itching and inflammation associated with itchy eyes.
Bisabolol: A sesquiterpene alcohol, bisabolol has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm irritated eyes and provide relief from itching.

Matricaria chamomilla flavonoids (including apigenin and luteolin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of apigenin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: They are used in combination with flowers to create a synergistic effect and enhance the cream's soothing and calming properties.
Seeds: Although less commonly used than flowers, seeds are also employed in some creams for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reliable source within a few months.
Combine the dried flowers with 250ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids within 30 minutes.
Whip 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter with 100ml of the infused oil for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Kansas coneflower cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and redness around the eye, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in the cream can neutralize free radicals and protect the delicate tissues around the eye from oxidative damage, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Soothing and calming effects: The topical application of Kansas coneflower cream can provide a soothing and calming effect on the skin around the eye, helping to reduce itchiness, irritation, and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The cream soothes and calms the irritated tissues around the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort. Its antihistamine effects also help in combating allergic reactions that may cause itchiness. Additionally, Echinacea angustifolia's natural anti-oxidant properties protect the delicate skin around the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting healing and relieving itchiness. This makes it a popular natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids, specifically echinacoside, have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Echinacea angustifolia have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing itchy sensations.

Alkylamides: Alkylamides in Echinacea angustifolia have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities, which can help alleviate itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in alkaloids and glycosides, which provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that can reduce itchy eye discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in the fall season after the plant has gone dormant for 6 weeks.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea angustifolia roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered echinacea angustifolia with 8 ounces of beeswax and 4 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture for 10 minutes until it thickens and forms a smooth creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain cream's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness in the eyes by minimizing the inflammation caused by irritants or allergic reactions.

Antihistamine-like effect: The compounds present in plantain cream have an antihistamine-like effect, which helps to relieve itching and burning sensations in the eyes by blocking histamine receptors and reducing the release of chemical mediators that contribute to itchiness.
Wound-healing and soothing properties: Plantain's wound-healing and soothing properties help to calm and protect the delicate eye tissues, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by itchy eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as aucubin and allantoin, calm irritated tissues and reduce swelling around the eyes. Plantain's natural antihistamine properties also help to alleviate itching and redness caused by allergies or environmental factors. The cream's moisturizing effects further soothe and protect the delicate skin around the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy eyes and promoting a comfortable, healthy eye environment.
remedy_constituents: Apolactone glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness.

Caffeic acid: As a phenolic compound, caffeic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate itching and redness in the eyes by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Plantaginis lactone: This lactone compound has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the eyes, thereby providing relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm itchy eyes.
Stems: Used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in itchy eyes.
Roots: Used due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and protect itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh plantago major leaves and flowers in the morning when dew is still present on the plants.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 30-40°c for 2-4 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 200g of dried plantago major material with 200g of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm water bath at 50-60°c for 2-4 hours to infuse the oil with plant properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Adiantum capillus-veneris extract: This extract has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by itchiness.
Quercetin: This flavonoid compound has natural antihistamine effects, which can relieve itching and burning sensations in the eyes associated with allergic reactions or eye irritants.
Astringent tannins: The tannin content in Maidenhair Tree creams helps to reduce excess moisture and discharge from the eyes, providing relief from itchy, watery, or gunky eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, soothe and calm the eye area, reducing redness and irritation. By promoting blood flow and oxygenation, Ginkgo biloba creams help to alleviate itchiness and discomfort associated with dry, irritated eyes. Additionally, its natural antibacterial properties help to prevent infections and promote a healthy environment around the eyes. This makes Ginkgo biloba creams a popular natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Bilobalide is a triterpene lactone that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchy eyes by soothing and calming the affected area.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to alleviate itchy eyes by reducing the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Flavone glycosides (e.g., kaempferol and isorhapontigenin): These flavone glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate itchy eyes by protecting the eye tissue from damage and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba in creams for itchy eyes because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: The seeds are also used in creams for itchy eyes because they contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic effects that help to reduce itching and swelling.

Barks: The barks are sometimes used in creams for itchy eyes because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to calm and soothe the itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 ounces of dried flowers at the peak of their potency.
Steep the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to separate the solids from the liquid.
Combine 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 10 to 15 minutes.
Mix the infused liquid with the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright creams containing anti-inflammatory herbs like chamomile, calendula, or licorice root can reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants present in herbal eyebright creams, such as vitamin E or beta-carotene, help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and itchiness, promoting overall eye health.

Soothing and calming effects: Eyebright creams infused with herbs like lavender, chamomile, or plantain have a calming effect on the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort by soothing and relaxing the ocular tissues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis, also known as eyebright, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal extract soothes and calms irritated eyes, reducing the urge to scratch. Eyebright contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, which can cause eye infections. By addressing the root causes of itchy eyes, these creams provide quick relief, promoting healthy and comfortable vision. Regular use can also help to prevent recurring eye irritations.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical mediator responsible for allergic reactions.
Leucosceptrine: This iridoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which contribute to its soothing and itching-relieving effects on the eyes.
Euphrasine: This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to calm and protect the eyes from irritation, redness, and itchiness.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers contain anthranoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, effectively reducing redness and itching associated with eye irritation.

Roots: The roots of Euphrasia officinalis contain a range of bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of eye irritation, making them a useful ingredient in creams for itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers at the peak of potency.
Dry the harvested material at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent degradation of the active compounds.
Infuse 5 grams of dried euphrasia officinalis in 100ml of cold-pressed coconut oil at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the herbal extract.
Mix 10% of the herbal extract with 90% of a suitable emulsifier and water base to create the cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness associated with itchy eyes.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in dandelion cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and irritation in the eyes, promoting healthy vision and reducing inflammation.
Anti-allergic properties: Dandelion's anti-allergic compounds help reduce allergic reactions and itching caused by environmental allergens, such as pollen or dust.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to calm and hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itchy and irritated eyes. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for itchy eye issues.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in dandelion creams, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthone, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in itchy eyes.
Terpenes (specifically, sesquiterpenes and triterpenes): Terpenes in dandelion creams, such as taraxasterol, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm itchy eyes.

Chlorogenic acid (a type of phenolic acid): Chlorogenic acid, a polyphenol found in dandelion, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and itching in itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Employed for their potential to reduce itching and inflammation, as well as their possible antiseptic properties that help prevent infections.
Roots: Utilized for their reported ability to reduce swelling and inflammation, which can provide relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 handfuls of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a clean and pesticide-free environment.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a warm place for 2-3 weeks.
Mix 20g of dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves with 100g of coconut oil in a glass jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 150-180°f for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and store the cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Antihistamines: The flavonoids present in peppermint can act as natural antihistamines, blocking histamine release and reducing itching, redness, and inflammation in the eyes.
Cicatrizant properties: Peppermint's cicatrizant properties help accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, which can aid in soothing itchy eyes caused by irritation or minor injuries.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and cooling properties. The menthol in peppermint creams provides a soothing sensation, reducing redness and discomfort. The herb's ability to relax muscles and reduce swelling makes it an effective remedy for itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or irritation. Additionally, peppermint's natural decongestant properties help to clear nasal passages, which can contribute to itchy eyes by reducing pressure and promoting drainage.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a compound that helps to numb the skin and eyes, providing temporary relief from itchy sensations by blocking the nerve endings.
Menthone: Menthone is a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and irritation that can cause itchy eyes.
Limonene: Limonene is a terpene that has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the skin and eyes, reducing the itching sensation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in oils and compounds that provide anti-inflammatory and soothing effects for itchy eyes.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita contain similar oils and compounds to the leaves, making them useful for relieving itchy eye symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used for their calming and soothing properties, which help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers at dawn to capture optimal oil content and aroma.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content and preserve potency.
Combine 20 grams of dried mentha x piperita with 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160-180°f for 10-15 minutes to melt the ingredients and combine the herbal extract with the oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and pour into a clean container to create the herbal cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help reduce swelling and itching in the eyes by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to itchy eyes, while also protecting the eyes from oxidative stress.
Cicatrisative effects: Rosemary's cicatrisative properties help to soothe and calm irritated eye tissue, reducing redness and discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in rosemary, such as carnosic acid, reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of rosemary help to prevent infection and soothe the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness. The soothing effect of rosemary creams can calm irritated eyes, making it an effective natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Carnosic acid, a phenolic diterpene, helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchy eyes by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic diterpene, aids in reducing inflammation and soothing itchy eyes by its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate, a monoterpene, helps to alleviate itchy eyes by its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide relief from eye discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in itchy eyes.
Stems: Stems are used due to their rich content of rosmarinic acid, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil in a 1:5 ratio for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to separate the solids.
Combine the infused oil with beeswax and shea butter in a 1:1:1 ratio to create a stable emulsion.
Heat the mixture over low heat while whisking constantly until the beeswax and shea butter are fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with itchy eyes because of its unique ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues. The extract from cornflowers contains powerful anti-inflammatory properties that quickly reduce redness and swelling, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, the moisturizing qualities of cornflower creams help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, reducing dryness and irritation that can exacerbate itchiness. As a result, users can enjoy a more comfortable and peaceful vision experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Cornflower's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in cornflower cream neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, which contributes to itchy eyes.
Sothing and moisturizing properties: Cornflower's soothing and moisturizing properties help calm irritated eyes, reduce itchiness, and restore the natural moisture balance of the eye area.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the flowers have natural antihistamine properties that reduce itching and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers of centaurea cyanus in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of cream.
Combine 200g of beeswax, 100ml of sweet almond oil, and 20ml of distilled water in a double boiler setup.
Add 10g of dried centaurea cyanus flowers to the mixture and heat it slowly until the wax is fully melted and combined.
Remove from heat and let cool to around 60°c before adding 5 drops of lavender essential oil for its calming properties.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars, allow it to cool completely and solidify at room temperature within 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal creams helps with itchy eyes because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. When applied topically to the affected area, berberine helps to reduce redness and swelling, while also combating bacterial and fungal infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, goldenseal's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe dry, irritated eyes, providing fast and effective relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which helps to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide is a flavonoid that has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to calm irritated eyes and reduce oxidative stress, alleviating itchiness and discomfort.

Berberastine: Berberastine is an alkaloid that has been found to possess antihistamine-like properties, which can help to counteract the release of histamine in the body, thereby reducing itching, redness, and inflammation associated with allergic reactions in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contains berbamine, a derivative of berberine that has been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dry, itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried goldenseal root powder hydrastis canadensis from reputable sources.
Combine the goldenseal powder with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heatproof mixing bowl.
Melt the mixture by placing it in a double boiler and heating it at 170 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool slightly before whipping it with a hand mixer or whisk until smooth and creamy.
Pour the herbal cream into clean glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: American witch hazel creams helps with itchy eyes because they contain a unique combination of natural ingredients that soothe and calm irritated eyelids. The witch hazel extract has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling, while the herbs like chamomile and calendula provide a gentle, non-irritating moisturizing effect. This blend helps to neutralize itchiness and discomfort, promoting healthy, comfortable eyes that are free from irritation and dryness. As a result, using American witch hazel creams can bring quick relief from itchy eyes, making them perfect for daily use.
remedy_properties: Tannins: Tannins in witch hazel cream help to reduce inflammation and itching by constricting blood vessels and reducing the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in witch hazel, such as gallotannic acid and gallic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help soothe and calm itchy eyes by reducing redness and swelling.

Volatile Oils: Witch hazel contains volatile oils like hamamelitannin, which has antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to itchiness and discomfort in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins, witch hazel roots are a natural anti-inflammatory and soothing agent that helps to reduce redness and itching.
Barks: The inner bark of the witch hazel plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce swelling and ease discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality hamamelis virginiana leaves and stems, ensuring freshness for optimal potency.
Combine one part dried hamamelis virginiana with two parts sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steam distill the mixture using a double boiler method for 30 minutes to extract essential oils.
Mix one tablespoon of the resulting essential oil with six tablespoons of coconut oil and beeswax, melting them together over low heat.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and use within three months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with itchy eyes because of their soothing and moisturizing properties. The natural ingredients in these creams, such as aloe vera and chamomile, calm irritated eyes and reduce inflammation. They also help to lubricate the eyes, relieving dryness and itchiness caused by allergies or environmental factors. By providing long-lasting relief, herbal marshmallow creams offer a gentle and effective solution for itchy, uncomfortable eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness in the eyes.
Soothing and coating property: The gel-like substance found in marshmallow roots helps to soothe and coat irritated eye tissues, providing instant relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antihistamine property: Marshmallow creams contain compounds that have antihistamine properties, which help alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergic reactions or environmental irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, providing instant relief from itchiness.
Barks: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds, the barks help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality marshmallow root (althaea officinalis) in the morning after dew.
Clean and dry 100g of dried marshmallow root in a clean glass jar for 24 hours.
Steep the dried marshmallow root in 500ml of boiling distilled water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids within 10 minutes.
Mix the cooled liquid with 100g of organic coconut oil and 50g of beeswax, heating until melted and blended.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Loquat creams helps with itchy eyes because of their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as loquat leaf extract, work to calm irritated conjunctiva and reduce redness. As a natural moisturizer, the cream also helps to hydrate the eyes, which can become dry and itchy due to environmental factors or allergies. By providing a comforting layer of protection, herbal loquat creams can bring instant relief to itchy, scratchy eyes, promoting comfort and ease throughout the day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Loquat cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in loquat cream, such as vitamin C and polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and exacerbate itchiness, promoting healthy eyes and reducing irritation.

Soothing demulcent properties: Loquat cream's demulcent properties provide a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, calming and soothing itchy, irritated eyes by locking in moisture and reducing dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: Contains flavonoids and tannins that provide a protective barrier against allergens and irritants, reducing itchiness and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eriobotrya japonica leaves, dry them thoroughly in the shade for at least three hours or overnight.
Grind dried eriobotrya japonica leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle, set aside two teaspoons of the powder.
Melt one hundred grams of petroleum jelly in a heat-resistant glass bowl over low heat, stirring occasionally, until smooth.
Add the ground eriobotrya japonica powder to the melted petroleum jelly, stir well for five minutes or until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly and allow it to cool at room temperature for two hours before storing in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with itchy eyes because of its unique blend of bioactive compounds, including allantoin and rosmarinic acid. These natural ingredients have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and itching. Additionally, comfrey's demulcent nature provides a protective barrier on the surface of the eye, shielding it from further irritation and promoting healthy tear production. This holistic approach to eye care can provide long-lasting relief for individuals suffering from dry, itchy, or irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin helps to soothe and calm irritated eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair, which can provide relief from itching and discomfort.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by irritation or infection, making them less itchy.

Allylsulfides: Allylsulfides have antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can help to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to itchiness in the eyes, promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots are rich in allantoin, a natural soothing agent that helps reduce inflammation and itchiness in the eyes.
Seeds: Comfrey seeds contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm irritated eye tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality symphytum officinale leaves and dry them completely within 24 hours of collection.
Grind 100 grams of dried symphytum officinale leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered symphytum officinale with 200 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes or until the beeswax has melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of 180°f to 190°f.
Pour the hot cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify within 30 minutes before labeling and storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chicory creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The natural ingredients in chicory, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, work to calm and reduce redness in the eyes. These compounds also help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, chicory creams can help to lubricate and moisturize the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itchy eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chicory extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eye, providing relief from itchiness caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or allergic reactions.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in chicory cream help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy eyes and promote healing.
Anti-allergic properties: Chicory extract has been found to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical mediator that contributes to allergic reactions and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chicory roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Chicory flowers contain flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 2 ounces of fresh cichorium intybus leaves in a warm oven for 30 minutes.
Combine the dried leaves with 8 ounces of distilled water in a stainless steel pot and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids immediately.
Add 1 ounce of sweet almond oil to the liquid and stir until fully incorporated over low heat for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars, sealing tightly and storing in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curly dock creams helps with itchy eyes because they contain soothing properties that calm irritated tissues. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin and taraxasterol, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing instant relief from itching. Additionally, curly dock's antimicrobial agents help to eliminate underlying infections or allergies that may be causing the itchiness. As a result, curly dock creams offer effective natural relief for itchy eyes, promoting comfort and visual clarity.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in Curly Dock cream can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to eye irritation and inflammation, promoting a healthy eye environment.
Soothing astringents: The natural astringent properties of Curly Dock can help constrict blood vessels and reduce discharge associated with itchy eyes, providing instant relief from itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
Rhyzomes: Contains salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh rumex crispus leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the leaves in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine one cup of dried rumex crispus leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 2-3 weeks, stirring occasionally, until the infusion is dark green.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 20 grams of beeswax and heat the mixture to 80 degrees celsius until the wax has melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hollyhock creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated eyes. The cream's natural ingredients, such as hollyhock extract, calm and reduce redness caused by dry eyes or allergies. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects help to hydrate the delicate skin around the eyes, relieving itchiness and discomfort. This natural solution provides long-lasting relief from itchy eyes, making it an effective and gentle alternative to synthetic treatments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Hollyhock cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant property of hollyhock cream protects the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to itchy eyes and dryness.
Softenng and lubricating property: Hollyhock cream's softening and lubricating properties help moisturize and soothe dry, irritated eyes, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and reducing redness in itchy eyes.
Bark: Contains flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-allergic properties, helping to calm itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh alcea rosea flowers and dry them in a cool dark place for at least 72 hours.
Combine 100g of dried alcea rosea flowers with 200ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 50ml of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a warm location for 4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to allow infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 10% beeswax and 2% vitamin e oil to the filtered oil and heat it gently in a double boiler until the mixture reaches a temperature of 120°f for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The natural oils present in fennel, such as anethole and fenchone, have a calming effect on the mucous membranes, reducing redness and irritation. When applied topically to the affected area, these creams provide instant relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to hydrate the eyes, further alleviating dryness and itchiness. This natural remedy provides a gentle and effective solution for individuals struggling with itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by allergies or irritants, providing relief from itchiness.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in fennel cream combat oxidative stress and free radical damage that can exacerbate itchy eyes, promoting healthy eye tissues and reducing inflammation.
Softenening and lubricating properties: Fennel's moisturizing effects help to soothe and calm dry, irritated eyes, making them less prone to itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eyes.
Seeds: Containing essential oils that can help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning when they are at their highest concentration of essential oils.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 4 hours and then store them in an airtight container.
Combine 50 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves with 200 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for itchy eyes are topical preparations derived from plants that help alleviate discomfort and irritation in the eyes. They work by soothing and calming the affected area, reducing inflammation and redness. The best herbal creams for itchy eyes include Aloe vera, Calendula officinalis, Symphytum officinale, Chamomilla recutita, Euphrasia officinalis, Euphorbia peplus, Plantago major, Echinacea purpurea, Hypericum perforatum, and Avena sativa. If left untreated, itchy eyes can lead to fatigue, decreased productivity, and even long-term vision problems, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal creams are various skin irritations and allergic reactions. For example, eczema, dermatitis, and rosacea can be soothed by topical herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil. These herbs have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling, providing relief from discomfort and promoting skin health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with itchy eyes is a blend of Aloe vera, Calendula, and Chamomile. Aloe vera soothes and moisturizes the skin around the eyes, while Calendula reduces inflammation and calms irritation. Chamomile, with its anti-inflammatory properties, further reduces redness and itching. This combination provides a gentle, natural solution to soothe itchy eyes and promote healing. Apply a thin layer of the cream to the affected area, and gently massage it in for optimal results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extract from Aloe vera contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes. When applied topically, Aloe vera cream can calm and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy sensations. Its natural cooling effect also helps to reduce irritation and discomfort, making it an effective remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: Help with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and soothing irritation due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Anthraquinones: Help with itchy eyes by reducing swelling and preventing the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching and allergic reactions.
Fatty acids: Help with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting healing through their anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of the gel-like substance used to soothe itchy eyes.
Gel from Leaves: Aloe vera gel is extracted from the leaves to create creams and ointments for itchy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Melt 1 cup of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a separate saucepan.
Add 1/4 cup of aloe vera gel to the saucepan and heat it gently.
Stir in 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 2 tablespoons of honey until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb's flavonoids and terpenoids work together to reduce redness and swelling, calming the delicate skin around the eyes. The cream's moisturizing effects also help to lock in moisture, providing long-lasting relief from dryness and itchiness. Additionally, calendula's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections, promoting a healthy environment for the eyes and surrounding skin. This natural remedy provides effective relief from itchy eyes without harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe the itchy and irritated eyes.
Naphthoquinones: They exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to prevent infections and reduce the severity of itchy eyes.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation, prevent further irritation, and promote the healing of the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, which help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, as they contain similar properties to the flowers, but to a lesser extent, making them a secondary choice for itchy eye creams.
Seeds: The seeds are occasionally used, although less frequently than the flowers and leaves, due to their potential anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help alleviate itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers when they are in full bloom for optimal potency and fragrance.
Dry the flowers in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Steep 1 part of dried calendula flowers in 2 parts of carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Strain the mixture after 2 weeks of infusion and discard the solids.
Whip the oil with beeswax and shea butter in a ratio of 1:1:1 to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with itchy eyes because they contain the active compound allantoin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream reduces swelling and redness in the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy and irritated eyes. The natural ingredients in Symphytum officinale cream also help to calm and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes, promoting a healthy and comfortable eye environment. As a result, itchy eyes can be effectively managed with Symphytum officinale cream.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help calm down the eye irritation and reduce the discomfort associated with itchy eyes.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help reduce redness and itching in the eyes by calming down the underlying inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain the highest concentration of allantoin, a key compound that soothes and calms itchy, irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots of Symphytum officinale contain a rich supply of mucilage, which helps to protect and moisturize the delicate skin around the eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of Symphytum officinale contribute to the cream's anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the symphytum officinale roots and leaves in a ratio of 3:2, dried them separately.
Mix 30 grams of dried symphytum officinale roots with 10 grams of dried leaves in a clean glass.
Combine 100 grams of beeswax, 100 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the dried herb mixture to the melted beeswax mixture, stir well for 10 minutes continuously.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal them immediately and store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream, derived from the flowers of the German chamomile plant, contains apigenin and luteolin, which calm and reduce swelling in the eyes. These flavonoids also have antihistamine effects, helping to alleviate the itching and redness associated with allergies or irritants. As a result, the cream provides quick relief from itchy eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid compound helps with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles around the eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Luteolin: This flavonoid constituent has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate itchy, irritated eyes by reducing swelling and redness.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects that help calm itchy, irritated eyes, promoting relaxation and comfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most used part due to their high content of apigenin and other flavonoids, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
Leaves: The leaves are another commonly used part due to their ability to calm and reduce inflammation in the eyes, making them effective in soothing itchy eyes.
Stems: The stems of German chamomile are sometimes used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of dried chamomilla recutita flowers and transfer them to a clean glass container.
Extract the plant material by steeping it in 200 milliliters of warm distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a separate container to separate the plant material.
Combine the extracted liquid with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture and stir until the wax has melted and the final product has cooled to room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb, also known as eye bright, contains flavonoids and tannins that calm and reduce inflammation in the eyes. The cream's cooling sensation and moisturizing effect also help to relieve dryness and irritation, providing instant relief from itchy eyes. By reducing inflammation and soothing the eyes, Euphrasia officinalis creams provide a natural solution to alleviate itchy eyes and promote overall eye health. This natural remedy is often used to treat conjunctivitis and other eye irritations.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the itchy and inflamed tissues around the eyes.
Phenolic Acids: As antioxidants, phenolic acids help to reduce inflammation and combat oxidative stress, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
Ursolic Acid: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to alleviate itchiness and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Used because of their antiseptic and astringent properties that help reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
Stems: Used due to their ability to provide a soothing and calming effect on itchy and irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 30g of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers and mix with 100ml of carrier oil.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 40-60°c for 4 hours to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth and discard the solids.
Mix 10ml of the infused oil with 10g of beeswax and 5ml of distilled water.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60-80°c for 10-15 minutes to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphorbia peplus creams helps with itchy eyes because they contain a high concentration of ingenol mebutate, a natural compound with potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. When applied topically, it can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes, providing quick relief from discomfort. The cream's ability to calm and comfort irritated skin and tissues around the eyes makes it an effective remedy for people suffering from itchy eyes due to various causes, including allergies, irritants, and dryness.
remedy_constituents: Cucurbitacin E: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe itchy eyes by reducing irritation and inflammation.
Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which help to reduce itching and inflammation in itchy eyes, promoting comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: The buds contain a high concentration of the active compound, ingenol mebutate, which is responsible for their healing and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: The stems of Euphorbia peplus contain the same active compound, ingenol mebutate, which is used to create creams to treat itchy eyes and other skin irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of euphorbia peplus leaves and stems at dawn when they are at their highest concentration of compound.
Dry the harvested material in a single layer at 40°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 100g of dried euphorbia peplus with 250g of sweet almond oil and heat the mixture to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10g of beeswax to the liquid.
Heat the mixture to 70°c for 10 minutes then pour it into a clean container and allow it to cool.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major creams helps with itchy eyes because they are rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, derived from the leaves of the Plantago major plant. The cream's active ingredients work to calm and reduce redness in the eyes, while also reducing itchiness and irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of the cream also help to alleviate any underlying swelling or puffiness, providing quick and effective relief from itchy eyes. This natural remedy is a popular alternative to harsh chemical-based eye creams.
remedy_constituents: Apolipoproteins: They help with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and soothing the affected area, which in turn reduces the irritation and discomfort.
Phenolic acids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to calm the eyes and reduce the itching sensation caused by allergic reactions or irritation.
Flavonoids: They act as an anti-inflammatory agent and have antihistamine properties, which help to reduce the itching, redness, and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Roots: The roots are used for their emollient and anti-itching properties, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Seeds: The seeds are used in creams to reduce inflammation and provide relief from itchy eyes due to their antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25g of fresh plantago major leaves on a sunny morning after dew has evaporated.
Steep the fresh leaves in 100ml of hot water at 80°c for 10 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth into a bowl to remove the solids instantly.
Combine 20g of beeswax with 20g of coconut oil in a saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Mix 5ml of the strained tea with 10g of aloe vera gel and 10g of the melted beeswax mixture slowly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active compounds, such as alkylamides and phenolic acids, work to reduce swelling and soothe irritated tissues in the eyes. This natural remedy can also help to calm the overactive immune response that contributes to allergic reactions and itchy eyes. As a result, Echinacea purpurea creams can provide quick and effective relief from itchy, irritated eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and calmness.
remedy_constituents: Icariin: This flavonoid constituent helps with itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the eyelid muscles, providing relief from discomfort.
Cichoric acid: This sesquiterpene lactone constituent helps with itchy eyes by inhibiting the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators, which contribute to itchy and watery eyes.

Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid constituent helps with itchy eyes by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, providing relief from itching.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their high content of alkaloids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are utilized because they contain echinacoside, a glycoside that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
Leaves: The leaves are often used due to their content of isobutylamides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers and leaves from the plant in the early morning.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer on paper bags for 7 to 10 days.
Create a herbal infusion by combining 2 tablespoons of dried flowers and leaves with 2 cups of boiling water.
Mix the cooled herbal infusion with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with itchy eyes because of its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties. The active ingredients in the cream, such as flavonoids and naphthodianthrones, work to reduce swelling and itching in the eyes. By soothing and calming the affected area, the cream provides relief from discomfort and irritation. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections and promote healing, making it a natural remedy for itchy eyes caused by allergies, dryness, or irritation. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness in itchy eyes.
Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol have potent anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, helping to alleviate itchiness and discomfort in the eyes.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly ferulic acid, possess antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation, thus providing relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain hyperforin, a compound that may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy eyes.
Flowers: Rich in hypericin and other antioxidants, they can help soothe and calm the affected area, reducing itching and discomfort.
Buds: They contain a combination of flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 10 grams of beeswax.
Combine the weighed ingredients with 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to remove any solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa creams helps with itchy eyes because they contain the soothing properties of oat kernel extract, which is rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The cream calms and cools the affected area, providing instant relief from itching and discomfort. Additionally, Avena sativa has been traditionally used to soothe irritated skin and mucous membranes, making it an effective remedy for itchy eyes caused by dryness, allergies, or other irritants. Regular use can help to reduce the frequency and severity of itchy eyes.
remedy_constituents: Avenanthramides: These antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and itching in itchy eyes by neutralizing free radicals and stabilizing the skin barrier.
Saponins: These surface-active compounds help soothe itchy eyes by reducing inflammation and irritation, while also promoting the healing of the ocular surface.

Fatty acids (including oleic and linoleic acids): These anti-inflammatory fatty acids help reduce redness and itching in itchy eyes by modulating the body's inflammatory response and promoting the production of anti-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots: The underground storage organs of Avena sativa, which are rich in saponins that help soothe itchy eyes.
Seeds: Seeds: The seeds of Avena sativa contain flavonoids and other compounds that reduce inflammation and itching associated with itchy eyes.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves of Avena sativa are used in creams for itchy eyes due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to calm irritated tissues.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried avena sativa in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl discarding the solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused avena sativa liquid and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the melted mixture.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
